item.dc:creator: Dan De Luce
Garrett Haake
Kelly O'Donnell
item.description: Tulsi Gabbard, the top-ranking U.S. intelligence official, told President Donald Trump on Friday that she is resigning from her role following her husband’s cancer diagnosis, according to her letter obtained by NBC News.

media:content.media:text: Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard testifies during a Senate Intelligence Committee hearing
media:content.media:description: Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard testifies during a Senate Intelligence Committee hearing on worldwide threats on Capitol Hill on March 18.

item.title: Tulsi Gabbard to resign as director of national intelligence

item.description: There is mounting frustration among some Republicans over President Donald Trump’s proposed spending, leading to both his $1 billion ballroom and the nearly $1.8 billion “anti-weaponization” fund hitting unexpected walls within the party. Objections over the latter fund prompted Republican senators to postpone a vote on an immigration bill and leave Capitol Hill for the week, according to two GOP sources. NBC’s Kelly O’Donnell reports for TODAY.

media:content.media:title: Republicans Cancel Votes Amid Trump’s ‘Anti-Weaponization’ Fund
media:content.media:description: There is mounting frustration among some Republicans over President Donald Trump’s proposed spending, leading to both his $1 billion ballroom and the nearly $1.8 billion “anti-weaponization” fund hitting unexpected walls within the party. Objections over the latter fund prompted Republican senators to postpone a vote on an immigration bill and leave Capitol Hill for the week, according to two GOP sources. NBC’s Kelly O’Donnell reports for TODAY.

item.title: Republicans Cancel Votes Amid Trump’s ‘Anti-Weaponization’ Fund

item.description: During a swearing-in ceremony at the White House, President Trump encouraged Kevin Warsh to be "totally independent" and encouraged him to "do your own thing" as the next chair of the Federal Reserve.

media:content.media:title: Trump tells Kevin Warsh to 'do your own thing' as Federal Reserve chair
media:content.media:description: During a swearing-in ceremony at the White House, President Trump encouraged Kevin Warsh to be "totally independent" and encouraged him to "do your own thing" as the next chair of the Federal Reserve.

item.title: Trump tells Kevin Warsh to 'do your own thing' as Federal Reserve chair

item.dc:creator: Mike Hixenbaugh
item.description: Long before Tony Waller, an Assemblies of God pastor, was convicted of raping girls, children told church leaders about hidden cameras, drugged drinks and abuse, a lawsuit says.

media:content.media:credit: Courtesy Stephanie Davis: Houston Cofield for NBC News
media:content.media:text: Stephanie Davis as a child, wearing the sash from the Assemblies of God's Missionettes girls program; Refuge Church of The Assemblies of God in Jonesboro, Ark.
media:content.media:description: Stephanie Davis as a child, wearing the sash from the Assemblies of God's Missionettes girls program; Refuge Church of The Assemblies of God in Jonesboro, Ark.

media:content.media:text: Tony Waller in court in Jonesboro, Ark., May 2015
media:content.media:description: Tony Waller was sentenced to life in prison in 2016.

media:content.media:text: "Unfortunately, nobody else had the courage to put their foot down,” Stephanie Davis said of failures to remove Tony Waller from ministry.
media:content.media:description: "Unfortunately, nobody else had the courage to put their foot down,” Stephanie Davis said of failures to remove Tony Waller from ministry.

media:content.media:text: The Assemblies of God headquarters in Springfield, Mo.
media:content.media:description: The Assemblies of God promotes child safety guidelines but has resisted making them mandatory.

media:content.media:text: Stephanie Davis stands outside the Refuge Church of the Assemblies of God
media:content.media:description: Five other women have signed on to Stephanie Davis' lawsuit.

item.title: Years of ignored warnings freed a children’s pastor to sexually abuse girls, lawsuit says

item.description: Watch highlights of the star-studded sendoff as Stephen Colbert brings the curtain down on 33 years of “The Late Show,” with legends like Jon Stewart, Paul McCartney, Bryan Cranston, and many surprises.

media:content.media:title: Watch highlights from Stephen Colbert's 'Late Show' finale
media:content.media:description: Watch highlights of the star-studded sendoff as Stephen Colbert brings the curtain down on 33 years of “The Late Show,” with legends like Jon Stewart, Paul McCartney, Bryan Cranston, and many surprises.

item.title: Watch highlights from Stephen Colbert's 'Late Show' finale

item.dc:creator: Daniella Silva
Julia Ainsley
item.description: The Trump administration said Friday that people with temporary visas seeking to adjust their immigration status to obtain green cards must return to their home countries to “do so through consular processing,” in a major change from current practice.

media:content.media:text: A woman leaves the U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services offices in New York
media:content.media:description: The change in the green card application process is the latest move by the Trump administration to target legal immigration.

item.title: Trump administration says visa holders seeking green cards must apply in home countries, in major change

item.dc:creator: Gretchen Morgenson
item.description: An independent investigation into the nation’s largest public pension, the California Public Employees’ Retirement System, has been completed.

media:content.media:text: USA News - 20 Jun 2022
media:content.media:description: The headquarters of the California Public Employees' Retirement System, or CalPERS, in Sacramento.

media:content.media:text: Margaret Brown.
media:content.media:description: The headquarters of the California Public Employees' Retirement System, or CalPERS, in Sacramento.

media:content.media:text: Rich Wiggins.
media:content.media:description: The headquarters of the California Public Employees' Retirement System, or CalPERS, in Sacramento.

item.title: Nation’s largest public pension fund plagued by secrecy and underperformance, probe finds

item.description: He was among the most winning drivers in NASCAR history and now the sudden death of Kyle Busch has left the racing world stunned. NBC News’ Sam Brock reports on what both NASCAR and Busch’s family have revealed about his death.

media:content.media:title: NASCAR star Kyle Busch dead at age 41
media:content.media:description: He was among the most winning drivers in NASCAR history and now the sudden death of Kyle Busch has left the racing world stunned. NBC News’ Sam Brock reports on what both NASCAR and Busch’s family have revealed about his death.

item.title: NASCAR star Kyle Busch dead at age 41

item.dc:creator: Harry Rabinowitz
item.description: Shop the best Memorial Day deals on mattresses, home appliances, beauty, tech and more from brands like Brooklinen, Ninja, Levoit, EltaMD and Apple.

media:content.media:credit: Amazon; Kara Birnbaum / NBC News; Brooklinen
media:content.media:text: It may not be Black Friday, but Memorial Day weekend is one of the best times to shop before summer hits.
media:content.media:description: It may not be Black Friday, but Memorial Day weekend is one of the best times to shop before summer hits.

media:content.media:text: It may not be Black Friday, but Memorial Day weekend is one of the best times to shop before summer hits.
media:content.media:description: It may not be Black Friday, but Memorial Day weekend is one of the best times to shop before summer hits.

item.title: The 93+ absolute best Memorial Day sales worth shopping

item.description: Watch live coverage as SpaceX launches the twelfth test flight of Starship. The company claims the third-generation prototype, dubbed V3, is larger, more powerful and a step closer to being fully reusable. The rocket is scheduled to liftoff from SpaceX's StarBase in Brownsville, Texas.

media:content.media:title: SpaceX launches prototype of Starship rocket in crucial test flight
media:content.media:description: Watch live coverage as SpaceX launches the twelfth test flight of Starship. The company claims the third-generation prototype, dubbed V3, is larger, more powerful and a step closer to being fully reusable. The rocket is scheduled to liftoff from SpaceX's StarBase in Brownsville, Texas.

item.title: SpaceX launches prototype of Starship rocket in crucial test flight

item.dc:creator: David K. Li
item.description: Oscar winner Lupita Nyong’o said she’s losing no sleep over racist criticism of her casting as Helen of Troy, the mythic beauty whose face “launched a thousand ships” and ignited the Trojan War

media:content.media:text: Lupita Nyong'o attends the Academy Museum Gala in Los Angeles on Oct. 18, 2025.
media:content.media:description: Lupita Nyong'o attends the Academy Museum Gala in Los Angeles on Oct. 18, 2025.

item.title: Lupita Nyong’o shrugs off racist ‘Odyssey’ backlash over Helen of Troy casting

item.dc:creator: Daniella Silva
Julia Ainsley
item.description: The Trump administration said Friday that people with temporary visas seeking to adjust their immigration status to obtain green cards must return to their home countries to “do so through consular processing,” in a major change from current practice.

media:content.media:text: A woman leaves the U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services offices in New York
media:content.media:description: The change in the green card application process is the latest move by the Trump administration to target legal immigration.

item.title: Trump administration says visa holders seeking green cards must apply in home countries, in major change

item.dc:creator: Dan De Luce
Garrett Haake
Kelly O'Donnell
item.description: Tulsi Gabbard, the top-ranking U.S. intelligence official, told President Donald Trump on Friday that she is resigning from her role following her husband’s cancer diagnosis, according to her letter obtained by NBC News.

media:content.media:text: Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard testifies during a Senate Intelligence Committee hearing
media:content.media:description: Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard testifies during a Senate Intelligence Committee hearing on worldwide threats on Capitol Hill on March 18.

item.title: Tulsi Gabbard to resign as director of national intelligence

item.dc:creator: Mike Hixenbaugh
item.description: Long before Tony Waller, an Assemblies of God pastor, was convicted of raping girls, children told church leaders about hidden cameras, drugged drinks and abuse, a lawsuit says.

media:content.media:text: Tony Waller in court in Jonesboro, Ark., May 2015
media:content.media:description: Tony Waller was sentenced to life in prison in 2016.

media:content.media:text: "Unfortunately, nobody else had the courage to put their foot down,” Stephanie Davis said of failures to remove Tony Waller from ministry.
media:content.media:description: "Unfortunately, nobody else had the courage to put their foot down,” Stephanie Davis said of failures to remove Tony Waller from ministry.

media:content.media:text: The Assemblies of God headquarters in Springfield, Mo.
media:content.media:description: The Assemblies of God promotes child safety guidelines but has resisted making them mandatory.

item.title: Years of ignored warnings freed a children’s pastor to sexually abuse girls, lawsuit says

item.dc:creator: Steve Kopack
item.description: Kevin Warsh was sworn in Friday as the new chair of the Federal Reserve, the world’s most influential central bank

media:content.media:text: President Trump Attends Swearing In Ceremony For New Chairman Of The Federal Reserve
media:content.media:description: Kevin Warsh shakes hands with President Trump after being sworn in as the new Chairman of the Federal Reserve at the White House on May 22, 2026.

media:content.media:text: President Donald Trump shakes hands with Kevin Warsh=
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump shakes hands with Kevin Warsh at Warsh's swearing-in ceremony Friday.

item.title: Kevin Warsh sworn in as Fed chair, but Trump’s rate cuts look increasingly unlikely

item.dc:creator: Francie Ebert
item.description: A Long Island woman was arrested last week for allegedly decapitating a statue of Jesus at a Catholic church

media:content.media:text: A Long Island woman is facing charges after allegedly taking the head off a statue of Jesus Christ at a Catholic church in East Islip.
media:content.media:description: A Long Island woman is facing charges after allegedly taking the head off a statue of Jesus Christ at a Catholic church in East Islip.

item.title: Long Island woman arrested after allegedly decapitating Jesus statue

item.dc:creator: Chantal Da Silva
item.description: British police urged witnesses to come forward on Friday as they continued to investigate potential offenses by former Prince Andrew related to Jeffrey Epstein.

media:content.media:text: King Charles III And Queen Camilla Attend The 2025 Easter Service At St George's Chapel
media:content.media:description: Prince Andrew, Duke of York attends the traditional Easter Sunday Mattins Service at St George's Chapel, Windsor Castle on April 20, 2025 in Windsor, England.

media:content.media:text: Virginia Giuffre (then Roberts) with Prince Andrew and Ghislaine Maxwell at Prince Andrew's London home in a photo released with court documents.
media:content.media:description: Virginia Giuffre (then Roberts) with the then-Prince Andrew and Jeffrey Epstein's confidant, Ghislaine Maxwell.

media:content.media:text: Jeffrey Epstein.
media:content.media:description: An undated photo of Jeffrey Epstein released by the Justice Department in 2025.

media:content.media:text: The State Funeral Of Queen Elizabeth II
media:content.media:description: Britain's King Charles III and his brother Andrew in London in 2022.

item.title: Police urge witnesses to come forward in former Prince Andrew investigation

item.dc:creator: Minyvonne Burke
item.description: A 12th hantavirus case has been confirmed after a crew member of the MV Hondius cruise ship, which arrived in the Netherlands last week for disinfection, tested positive, the World Health Organization said Friday.

media:content.media:text: MV Hondius Cruise Ship In Quarantine In The Netherlands
media:content.media:description: The MV Hondius docked in Rotterdam, Netherlands on May 20, 2026.

item.title: Hantavirus cases linked to cruise ship rise to 12 after crew member tests positive

item.dc:creator: Andrew Greif
item.description: Competing while using banned substances is one of elite sports’ most fiercely protected red lines.

media:content.media:text: Fred Kerley competes in the men's 100m during the IAAF Wanda Diamond League: Golden Gala Pietro Mennea in Rome, Italy, in June 2025.
media:content.media:description: Fred Kerley competes in the men's 100 meters during the IAAF Wanda Diamond League: Golden Gala Pietro Mennea in Rome in June.

media:content.media:text: Shane Ryan of Team Ireland during a training session at the 2024 Summer Olympic Games in Paris, France in July 2024.
media:content.media:description: Fred Kerley competes in the men's 100 meters during the IAAF Wanda Diamond League: Golden Gala Pietro Mennea in Rome in June.

item.title: A new sporting event’s controversial pitch: Performance-enhancing drugs welcome

item.dc:creator: Gretchen Morgenson
item.description: An independent investigation into the nation’s largest public pension, the California Public Employees’ Retirement System, has been completed.

media:content.media:text: USA News - 20 Jun 2022
media:content.media:description: The headquarters of the California Public Employees' Retirement System, or CalPERS, in Sacramento.

media:content.media:text: Margaret Brown.
media:content.media:description: The headquarters of the California Public Employees' Retirement System, or CalPERS, in Sacramento.

media:content.media:text: Rich Wiggins.
media:content.media:description: The headquarters of the California Public Employees' Retirement System, or CalPERS, in Sacramento.

item.title: Nation’s largest public pension fund plagued by secrecy and underperformance, probe finds

item.dc:creator: Kyle Stewart
Jennifer Jett
item.description: House Republicans delayed a vote on a war powers resolution that would restrict President Donald Trump’s ability to continue U.S. military operations in Iran.

media:content.media:text: Iran-Music Ceremony On Ruins Of Destroyed Synagogue
media:content.media:description: An unidentified man places an Iranian national flag on the ruins of a synagogue in Tehran, Iran, on May 14, 2026.

item.title: House Republicans cancel vote curbing Trump on Iran

item.dc:creator: Natasha Korecki
Ben Kamisar
item.description: The Democratic National Committee plunged into a fresh round of chaos after Chair Ken Martin was forced to release an autopsy report he commissioned about the failed 2024 presidential campaign.

media:content.media:text: Ken Martin is partially obscured by a heavily-blurred foreground.
media:content.media:description: Ken Martin, chairman of the Democratic National Committee, following a press conference with Texas Democrats at the International Union of Painters and Allied Trades union hall in Aurora, Ill. on Aug. 5, 2025.

item.title: Pressure builds on DNC chair as questions remain on handling of 2024 autopsy

item.dc:creator: Laura Strickler
Julia Ainsley
Helen Graham
item.description: Suicides rise in ICE detention; 911 calls detail serious cases of self-harm

media:content.media:text: South Texas ICE Processing Center.
media:content.media:description: South Texas ICE Processing Center.

media:content.media:text: A Colorado Springs community group gathered outside City Hall on March 12, calling on elected officials to help secure the release of Colorado Springs mother Hayam El Gamal and her five children, who have been held in a federal detention center since June.
media:content.media:description: A Colorado Springs community group gathered outside City Hall on March 12, calling on elected officials to help secure the release of Colorado Springs mother Hayam El Gamal and her five children, who have been held in a federal detention center since June.

media:content.media:text: US-JUSTICE-IMMIGRATION
media:content.media:description: ICE agents look over lists of people's names and their hearing times and locations at the Federal Plaza courthouse in New York City before making arrests on June 27.

media:content.media:text: Victor Manuel Diaz's mother and son at his funeral.
media:content.media:description: Victor Manuel Diaz's mother and son at his funeral.

item.title: Suicides rise in ICE detention; 911 calls detail serious cases of self-harm

item.dc:creator: Nicole Acevedo
Will Ujek
item.description: The Justice Department said the defendants defrauded Minnesota Medicaid and other programs of more than $90 million, and two of them billed for autism services that were never provided.

media:content.media:text: Colin McDonald gestures with both hands while speaking at a lectern. RFK Jr. is visible behind him.
media:content.media:description: Assistant Attorney General for the National Fraud Enforcement Division Colin McDonald addresses media to announce actions for combating fraud in Minnesota at the U.S. Attorney's Office for the District of Minnesota in Minneapolis, on May 21, 2026.

item.title: Minnesota autism, disabled services providers among 15 charged with Medicaid fraud

item.dc:creator: Daniel Arkin
Allan Smith
item.description: New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani plans to launch a livestream series called “Talk with the People”

media:content.media:text: New York Mayor-elect Zohran Mamdani at his transition office
media:content.media:description: New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani is launching a livestream series on Twitch.

item.title: NYC Mayor Zohran Mamdani is launching a chat show on Twitch
